name: Koyl's Reanimated Maw
oracle_text: Deathtouch\n{2}{B}, {T}: Overclock. (Exile the top three cards of your library. You may play them until end of turn.) Whenever ~ overclocks, each opponent loses 2 life.
flavor_text: The Vizier's collection of severed heads grows daily. Most are useful only once. This one... this one keeps talking.

validation_errors: Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(Exile the top three cards of your library. You may play them until end of turn.)"

art_prompt: A grotesque severed head mounted on a mechanical construct, its pale features twisted in a permanent snarl. Exposed brain tissue glistens beneath translucent skull-plates fused with corroded brass and iron. Multiple eyes—some human, some not—bulge from asymmetric sockets. Wet sinew wraps around articulated metal joints. Deathly aura emanates from its form.. Stylized digital illustration, heavy shadows with minimal light, organic textures and wet surfaces, unsettling asymmetry, post-apocalyptic science-fantasy setting.
design_notes: This card is a black mythic that delivers on the Anomalous Descent theme: a horrific construct piloted by an extracted brain, capable of siphoning knowledge (overclock) and draining life from enemies. At CMC 4 with a 4/4 body and deathtouch, it's efficient and threatening. The repeatable overclock activation ({2}{B}, {T}) is expensive enough to prevent infinite loops while remaining impactful—each activation draws 3 cards and drains 2 life, creating a genuine advantage sink. This fits Koyl Yrenum's grotesque legacy and the set's science-horror aesthetic. Mechanical design respects the overclock cost structure: the reminder text appears on first use, and the triggered drain effect ("Whenever ~ overclocks") creates a meaningful reward for the activation. The deathtouch ensures the body matters even without the ability, and the Legendary status makes it a signature card for black control/midrange strategies. Power level: slightly above rate for a 4/4 with deathtouch at mythic, but the overclock activation requires both tapping and paying mana, balancing the threat. Fits the BR (Raider Warbands) and UB (Deep Descent) archetypes, giving black a powerful sink for excess mana in the late game."
